claim: Before interstate moves, movers are required to provide the "Your Rights and Responsibilities When You Move" booklet and FMCSA's "Ready to Move" brochure.
usage_guidance: Safe to use as baseline consumer-rights context for legitimate interstate moving.

claim: Operation Protect Your Move is a nationwide FMCSA enforcement initiative aimed at household-goods carriers and brokers with egregious complaint histories and serious safety deficiencies.
usage_guidance: Safe to use when explaining why regulators focus on rogue movers and complaint-heavy brokers.

claim: From April 1 through April 19, 2024, FMCSA said it investigated companies in 17 states in response to a significant increase in household-goods hostage complaints.
usage_guidance: Use as a dated enforcement datapoint and attribute directly to FMCSA.

claim: DOT OIG says moving fraud can include low-ball estimates followed by threats to withhold household goods unless the customer pays significantly more, along with false weights or volumes and fraudulent bills of lading.
source_title: Household Goods Moving Fraud | DOT OIG
usage_guidance: Safe for explaining the scam playbook from an official investigative source.

claim: In July 2018, a federal indictment in the Southern District of Ohio charged 12 people in a moving-company racketeering scheme, and DOJ said more than 900 customers had been identified as victims at that stage.
source_title: Twelve Charged in Moving Company Scams | U.S. DOJ SD Ohio
usage_guidance: Use only with charged/alleged wording because the release describes an indictment and alleged conduct.

claim: On August 28, 2024, DOJ said Andre Prince was sentenced to 24 months in prison and ordered to pay $100,000 in forfeiture after his conviction in a fraudulent moving-company scheme.
source_title: Employee of Brooklyn Moving Company Sentenced for Defrauding Customers | U.S. DOJ EDNY
usage_guidance: Safe to describe as convicted and sentenced because the DOJ release states both.

claim: BBB said that in 2024 there were more than 100,000 inquiries on BBB.org about movers, 718 complaints filed against movers, and a median reported loss of $754 among moving-scam victims who reported to BBB Scam Tracker.
usage_guidance: Use as consumer-complaint context and attribute to BBB rather than presenting it as government data.

claim: Competition Bureau Canada warned that rogue movers may quote a low price, promise no surprise fees, present a different contract on moving day, add charges like warehousing and pickup fees, and in some instances hold possessions hostage until more money is paid.
source_url: dodge the rogue movers who take your belongings hostage | Competition Bureau Canada
column12: Safe to use as regulator-described scam mechanics with attribution to Competition Bureau Canada.

claim: The Government of Canada's Office of Consumer Affairs says long-distance moves require an inventory before transporting belongings, with items tagged and listed by the mover and a copy supplied to the customer.
source_title: Moving advice | Office of Consumer Affairs Canada
usage_guidance: Useful for a legitimate-mover checklist and for contrasting normal process with scam behavior.
